title: Eria meirax
taxonomy: Eria meirax is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Eria and the family Orchidaceae. The genus Eria is a group of terrestrial orchids, commonly found in tropical regions. Eria meirax is part of the subtribe Myrmecliadinae and tribe Cranichideae. This classification places Eria meirax within a specific phylogenetic group within the orchid family. Further classification may be refined through ongoing botanical research and taxonomy.

intro: Eria meirax, also known as Meirax orchid or Meirax root, is a plant species belonging to the Orchidaceae family, traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Eria meirax include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Eria meirax include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Eria meirax is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and other health ailments.
